channel.title: NPR Topics: News
channel.description: NPR news, audio, and podcasts. Coverage of breaking stories, national and world news, politics, business, science, technology, and extended coverage of major national and world events.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: 'Taiwan Travelogue' wins the 2026 International Booker Prize
item.description: The novel is the first work translated from Mandarin Chinese to win the award, which celebrates its 10th anniversary this year.
item.content:encoded:
The novel is the first work translated from Mandarin Chinese to win the award, which celebrates its 10th anniversary this year.

(Image credit: Adrian Dennis)
item.dc:creator: Ivy Buck

item.title: Trump endorses Ken Paxton over incumbent John Cornyn in Texas Senate primary runoff
item.description: President Trump has endorsed Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton over incumbent Sen. John Cornyn in the Texas GOP runoff for U.S. Senate, one week before voting ends in the contentious and expensive primary.
item.content:encoded:
President Trump has endorsed Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton over incumbent Sen. John Cornyn in the Texas GOP runoff for U.S. Senate, one week before voting ends in the contentious and expensive primary.

(Image credit: Greg Nash)
item.dc:creator: Stephen Fowler

item.title: Minnesota becomes first state to ban prediction markets
item.description: While dozens of states have taken legal action against the controversial industry, Minnesota is the first state to pass a law making it a felony for companies like Kalshi and Polymarket to operate.
item.content:encoded:
While dozens of states have taken legal action against the controversial industry, Minnesota is the first state to pass a law making it a felony for companies like Kalshi and Polymarket to operate.

(Image credit: Steve Karnowski)
item.dc:creator: Bobby Allyn

item.title: States sue over new student loan limits on certain nursing and healthcare degrees
item.description: New York, Arizona, North Carolina, Kentucky and Nevada are among the states challenging a rule that limits federal student loans for graduate degrees in nursing, physical therapy and more.
item.content:encoded:
New York, Arizona, North Carolina, Kentucky and Nevada are among the states challenging a rule that limits federal student loans for graduate degrees in nursing, physical therapy and more.

(Image credit: kieferpix)
item.dc:creator: Cory Turner

item.title: Can't keep a habit? This comic shares a proven formula to make it stick
item.description: In this illustrated guide, behavioral scientist BJ Fogg breaks down his Tiny Habits framework to help you rethink your approach to starting (or restarting) a habit.
item.content:encoded: In this illustrated guide, behavioral scientist BJ Fogg breaks down his Tiny Habits framework to help you rethink your approach to starting (or restarting) a habit.
item.dc:creator: Marielle Segarra

item.title: A study investigates: Did the abrupt end of USAID have an impact on violence?
item.description: That's the provocative question that researchers dug into after the U.S. shut down its premier aid agency.
item.content:encoded:
That's the provocative question that researchers dug into after the U.S. shut down its premier aid agency.

(Image credit: Andrew Kasuku/AP)
item.dc:creator: Ari Daniel

item.title: Israel's trying to expel a whole Palestinian district in East Jerusalem, activists say
item.description: Israeli authorities are issuing Palestinians demolition orders in East Jerusalem at an accelerated rate since Israel launched war with Iran, human rights groups and U.N. experts say.
item.content:encoded:
Israeli authorities are issuing Palestinians demolition orders in East Jerusalem at an accelerated rate since Israel launched war with Iran, human rights groups and U.N. experts say.

(Image credit: Ammar Awad)
item.dc:creator: Eleanor Beardsley

item.title: To revive an extinct bird, you first need an artificial egg
item.description: Colossal Biosciences, a Texas company trying to bring extinct species back to life, reports creating artificial eggs that would be necessary to revive extinct birds such as the dodo.
item.content:encoded:
Colossal Biosciences, a Texas company trying to bring extinct species back to life, reports creating artificial eggs that would be necessary to revive extinct birds such as the dodo.

(Image credit: Colossal Biosciences)
item.dc:creator: Rob Stein

item.title: California mosque shooting leaves 5 dead. And, judge dismisses Trump's IRS lawsuit
item.description: San Diego authorities are investigating a deadly shooting at a mosque as a hate crime. And, Trump dropped his lawsuit against the IRS, paving the way for an "anti-weaponization fund."
item.content:encoded:
San Diego authorities are investigating a deadly shooting at a mosque as a hate crime. And, Trump dropped his lawsuit against the IRS, paving the way for an "anti-weaponization fund."

(Image credit: Zoe Meyers)
item.dc:creator: Brittney Melton

item.title: Some plants have a genetic superpower that may help them survive a cataclysm
item.description: Get ready for a biology lesson. Certain plants have extra sets of chromosomes. And it turns out, it's a useful trait for a species facing a dramatic event like climate change.
item.content:encoded:
Get ready for a biology lesson. Certain plants have extra sets of chromosomes. And it turns out, it's a useful trait for a species facing a dramatic event like climate change.

(Image credit: Wolfgang Kaehler/LightRocket)
item.dc:creator: Ari Daniel
